addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## project
Project management CLI — init, list, doctor, status, lint, sync, register, history, templates, resume. Use project_init to scaffold new projects with AI-powered defaults. Use project_doctor for a full project intelligence report. Use project_list to see all projects across machines. Use project_sync with all=true to register all ~/Projects dirs in TrailBase. Use project_resume to continue interrupted sessions (e.g. after timeout).

attachment.content:
- ax: This skill helps with using the @ax-llm/ax TypeScript library for building LLM applications. Use when the user asks about ax(), ai(), f(), s(), agent(), flow(), AxGen, AxAgent, AxFlow, signatures, streaming, or mentions @ax-llm/ax.
- ax-agent: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxAgent code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about agent(), child agents, namespaced functions, discovery mode, shared fields, llmQuery(...), RLM code execution, recursionOptions, or agent runtime behavior. For tuning and eval with agent.optimize(...), use ax-agent-optimize.
- ax-agent-optimize: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxAgent tuning and evaluation code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about agent.optimize(...), judgeOptions, eval datasets, optimization targets, saved optimizedProgram artifacts, or recursive optimization guidance.
- ax-ai: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AI provider setup and configuration code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about ai(), providers, models, presets, embeddings, extended thinking, context caching, or mentions OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Azure/Groq/DeepSeek/Mistral/Cohere/Together/Ollama/HuggingFace/Reka/OpenRouter with @ax-llm/ax.
- ax-flow: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxFlow workflow code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about flow(), AxFlow, workflow orchestration, parallel execution, DAG workflows, conditional routing, map/reduce patterns, or multi-node AI pipelines.
- ax-gen
- ax-gepa

- ax

- ax-ai

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review

# Fix the generate-image flow

## Context

The `generate_image` browser tool (`web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`) currently:

- **Hardcodes** OpenAI as the only real image backend (`gpt-image-1.5`, endpoint `api.openai.com/v1/images/generations`) inside `generateOpenAiImage`. There is no setting to choose a generator, and the model id lives inline in the tool rather than in a central place.
- **Falls back to hand-built SVG "templates"** (`buildLocalImageSvg` → `buildInfographicSvg` / `buildAnatomySvg` / `buildComparisonSvg` / `buildProcessSvg`) whenever no key is configured or `mode` is `auto`/`svg`. The user considers these template SVGs useless.

Goal:
1. Add a setting to pick the image generator — **local (a local OpenAI‑compatible endpoint)** or **remote (OpenAI)** — with all model choices defined in one central module.
2. Remove the SVG template generators entirely. When no image generator is available/configured, `generate_image` returns a **plain message** (no image, no placeholder).

Decisions confirmed with user:
- "Local" = a configurable **local OpenAI‑compatible** `/images/generations` endpoint (reuses existing proxy plumbing).
- No-model case = **plain text result**, no SVG.

## 1. Central image-generator config — new file `web/src/lib/image-generators.ts`

Single source of truth for image backends + their model choices. Mirrors the style of `provider-models.ts`.

```ts
export type ImageGeneratorId = "openai" | "local";

export interface ImageGeneratorOption {
  id: ImageGeneratorId;
  label: string;
  description: string;
  // "openai": fixed remote endpoint + provider key "openai"
  // "local":  base URL comes from settings.localImageBaseUrl
  fixedEndpoint?: string;       // openai -> https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations
  providerKey?: string;         // provider name for getProviderApiKey()  (openai -> "openai", local -> "local-image")
  models: string[];             // first entry = default
  sizes: string[];              // first = default
  qualities: string[];          // first = default
}

export const IMAGE_GENERATORS: ImageGeneratorOption[] = [
  { id: "openai", label: "OpenAI", ..., fixedEndpoint: ".../v1/images/generations", providerKey: "openai",
    models: ["gpt-image-1.5", "gpt-image-1", "gpt-image-1-mini"], sizes: ["1024x1024","1536x1024","1024x1536"], qualities: ["medium","low","high"] },
  { id: "local", label: "Local (OpenAI-compatible)", ..., providerKey: "local-image",
    models: [], sizes: [...], qualities: [...] },  // models discovered/free-form; default model from settings.imageModel
];

export function getImageGenerator(id: string): ImageGeneratorOption | undefined { ... }
export const DEFAULT_IMAGE_GENERATOR_ID: ImageGeneratorId = "openai";
```

The `local` endpoint is built from the base URL: `trimTrailingSlash(baseUrl)` + (`/v1/images/generations` or `/images/generations` if base already ends in `/v1`). Reuse the trailing-slash + `/v1` normalization logic already present in `provider-models.ts` (`trimTrailingSlash`, `openAiCompatibleBaseUrls` pattern).

## 2. Settings — `web/src/keating/ui-settings.ts`

Add to `KeatingUiSettings`, `DEFAULT_UI_SETTINGS`, and `normalizeSettings()`:

- `imageGenerator: ImageGeneratorId` (default `"openai"`)
- `imageModel: string` (default `""` → tool resolves to generator's first model)
- `imageSize: string` (default `""`)
- `imageQuality: string` (default `""`)
- `localImageBaseUrl: string` (default `""`)

Normalize `imageGenerator` against `IMAGE_GENERATORS` ids; coerce the rest to strings. No new event plumbing needed — existing `saveKeatingUiSettings` / `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` already broadcast.

## 3. Settings UI — `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`

Add an "Image generation" section:
- Select for **generator** (maps over `IMAGE_GENERATORS`).
- Select for **model** (options = selected generator's `models`; for `local` with empty models, a free-text input).
- Text input for **Local endpoint base URL**, shown only when `imageGenerator === "local"` (e.g. `http://localhost:1234`).
- Optional selects for size / quality.

Follow the existing controlled-select pattern in this tab (it already renders selects bound to `loadKeatingUiSettings()` and calls `saveKeatingUiSettings`). The local API key (if the endpoint needs one) is entered through the existing Providers key storage under name `local-image` via `getProviderApiKey`/`providerKeys`; note this in the field helper text. (If a key field is desired inline, reuse `getAppStorage().providerKeys.set("local-image", ...)`.)

## 4. Rework the tool — `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`

**Delete** (and their only-internal helpers, confirmed unused elsewhere except test re-exports):
- `wrapSvgText`, `svgTextLines`
- `buildInfographicSvg`, `buildAnatomySvg`, `buildComparisonSvg`, `buildProcessSvg`, `buildLocalImageSvg`
- The test re-exports `__test_buildProcessSvg`, `__test_buildLocalImageSvg`, `__test_buildInfographicSvg` (bottom of file, ~line 2190). Grep confirms no test file references them.

**Generalize** `generateOpenAiImage` → `generateImageViaEndpoint({ endpoint, apiKey, model, size, quality, prompt })`: same fetch/proxy logic (`proxiedProviderRequestUrl`, bearer header, `b64_json` extraction), but endpoint + key are passed in instead of hardcoded.

**Rewrite the `generate_image` tool body** (~lines 1106-1198):
- Keep the authored-content guard (reject generic title/subtitle/<3 points) — still desirable.
- Drop the `mode` (`auto`/`svg`/`model`) param and all SVG branches. Keep `kind`/`labels`/`points` only as prompt enrichment.
- Resolve generator from settings: `const gen = getImageGenerator(loadKeatingUiSettings().imageGenerator)`.
- Resolve endpoint + apiKey:
  - `openai`: `gen.fixedEndpoint`, key = `getProviderApiKey("openai")`.
  - `local`: build endpoint from `settings.localImageBaseUrl`; key = `getProviderApiKey("local-image")` (may be empty for keyless local servers).
- **No-model guard** → return a plain string (no `<keating-image>`):
  - openai with no key, OR local with no base URL → e.g. `"No image generation model is available. Choose and configure one in Settings → Image generation (the local endpoint needs a base URL; OpenAI needs an API key)."`
- Otherwise call `generateImageViaEndpoint`, return the existing `<keating-image json=... />` payload with `dataUrl`. On a thrown request error, surface the error text as a plain message (no SVG fallback).
- Update the tool **description** string to drop SVG/template wording and describe generator selection.

`GeneratedImageCard` (`AssistantChatPanel.tsx`) still accepts `svg` for backward compatibility — leave it; we simply stop producing `svg`.

## 5. Regenerate the nodepod boot snapshot

`web/src/keating/nodepod-boot-files.ts` is **auto-generated** and embeds a compiled copy of `browser-tools`. After the edits run:

```
just generate-nodepod-boot     # = bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts
```

## Verification

- `cd web && bun run typecheck` (or the repo's tsc script) — confirm removed helpers/exports leave no dangling refs.
- `cd web && bun test` — existing browser-tools tests still pass (none reference the deleted SVG builders; verified by grep).
- Manual (`/run` or `bun dev`):
  1. Settings → Image generation: switch generator to **Local**, set a bogus base URL empty → ask the assistant to generate an image → expect the plain "no image generator available" message, **no SVG card**.
  2. Set generator **OpenAI** with no key → same plain message.
  3. With a valid OpenAI key (or a local ComfyUI/LM-style `/images/generations` server) → expect a real raster image card.
- Confirm the model/size/quality choices all originate from `image-generators.ts` (grep for `gpt-image` should only appear in that one module).

1. Add a setting to pick the image generator — **local (a local OpenAI‑compatible endpoint)** or **remote (OpenAI)** — with all model choices defined in one central module.
2. Remove the SVG template generators entirely. When no image generator is available/configured, `generate_image` returns a **plain message** (no image, no placeholder).

Decisions confirmed with user:
- "Local" = a configurable **local OpenAI‑compatible** `/images/generations` endpoint (reuses existing proxy plumbing).
- No-model case = **plain text result**, no SVG.

## 1. Central image-generator config — new file `web/src/lib/image-generators.ts`

Single source of truth for image backends + their model choices. Mirrors the style of `provider-models.ts`.

```ts
export type ImageGeneratorId = "openai" | "local";

export interface ImageGeneratorOption {
  id: ImageGeneratorId;
  label: string;
  description: string;
  // "openai": fixed remote endpoint + provider key "openai"
  // "local":  base URL comes from settings.localImageBaseUrl
  fixedEndpoint?: string;       // openai -> https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations
  providerKey?: string;         // provider name for getProviderApiKey()  (openai -> "openai", local -> "local-image")
  models: string[];             // first entry = default
  sizes: string[];              // first = default
  qualities: string[];          // first = default
}

export const IMAGE_GENERATORS: ImageGeneratorOption[] = [
  { id: "openai", label: "OpenAI", ..., fixedEndpoint: ".../v1/images/generations", providerKey: "openai",
    models: ["gpt-image-1.5", "gpt-image-1", "gpt-image-1-mini"], sizes: ["1024x1024","1536x1024","1024x1536"], qualities: ["medium","low","high"] },
  { id: "local", label: "Local (OpenAI-compatible)", ..., providerKey: "local-image",
    models: [], sizes: [...], qualities: [...] },  // models discovered/free-form; default model from settings.imageModel
];

export function getImageGenerator(id: string): ImageGeneratorOption | undefined { ... }
export const DEFAULT_IMAGE_GENERATOR_ID: ImageGeneratorId = "openai";
```

The `local` endpoint is built from the base URL: `trimTrailingSlash(baseUrl)` + (`/v1/images/generations` or `/images/generations` if base already ends in `/v1`). Reuse the trailing-slash + `/v1` normalization logic already present in `provider-models.ts` (`trimTrailingSlash`, `openAiCompatibleBaseUrls` pattern).

## 2. Settings — `web/src/keating/ui-settings.ts`

Add to `KeatingUiSettings`, `DEFAULT_UI_SETTINGS`, and `normalizeSettings()`:

- `imageGenerator: ImageGeneratorId` (default `"openai"`)
- `imageModel: string` (default `""` → tool resolves to generator's first model)
- `imageSize: string` (default `""`)
- `imageQuality: string` (default `""`)
- `localImageBaseUrl: string` (default `""`)

Normalize `imageGenerator` against `IMAGE_GENERATORS` ids; coerce the rest to strings. No new event plumbing needed — existing `saveKeatingUiSettings` / `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` already broadcast.

## 3. Settings UI — `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`

Add an "Image generation" section:
- Select for **generator** (maps over `IMAGE_GENERATORS`).
- Select for **model** (options = selected generator's `models`; for `local` with empty models, a free-text input).
- Text input for **Local endpoint base URL**, shown only when `imageGenerator === "local"` (e.g. `http://localhost:1234`).
- Optional selects for size / quality.

Follow the existing controlled-select pattern in this tab (it already renders selects bound to `loadKeatingUiSettings()` and calls `saveKeatingUiSettings`). The local API key (if the endpoint needs one) is entered through the existing Providers key storage under name `local-image` via `getProviderApiKey`/`providerKeys`; note this in the field helper text. (If a key field is desired inline, reuse `getAppStorage().providerKeys.set("local-image", ...)`.)

## 4. Rework the tool — `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`

**Delete** (and their only-internal helpers, confirmed unused elsewhere except test re-exports):
- `wrapSvgText`, `svgTextLines`
- `buildInfographicSvg`, `buildAnatomySvg`, `buildComparisonSvg`, `buildProcessSvg`, `buildLocalImageSvg`
- The test re-exports `__test_buildProcessSvg`, `__test_buildLocalImageSvg`, `__test_buildInfographicSvg` (bottom of file, ~line 2190). Grep confirms no test file references them.

**Generalize** `generateOpenAiImage` → `generateImageViaEndpoint({ endpoint, apiKey, model, size, quality, prompt })`: same fetch/proxy logic (`proxiedProviderRequestUrl`, bearer header, `b64_json` extraction), but endpoint + key are passed in instead of hardcoded.

**Rewrite the `generate_image` tool body** (~lines 1106-1198):
- Keep the authored-content guard (reject generic title/subtitle/<3 points) — still desirable.
- Drop the `mode` (`auto`/`svg`/`model`) param and all SVG branches. Keep `kind`/`labels`/`points` only as prompt enrichment.
- Resolve generator from settings: `const gen = getImageGenerator(loadKeatingUiSettings().imageGenerator)`.
- Resolve endpoint + apiKey:
  - `openai`: `gen.fixedEndpoint`, key = `getProviderApiKey("openai")`.
  - `local`: build endpoint from `settings.localImageBaseUrl`; key = `getProviderApiKey("local-image")` (may be empty for keyless local servers).
- **No-model guard** → return a plain string (no `<keating-image>`):
  - openai with no key, OR local with no base URL → e.g. `"No image generation model is available. Choose and configure one in Settings → Image generation (the local endpoint needs a base URL; OpenAI needs an API key)."`
- Otherwise call `generateImageViaEndpoint`, return the existing `<keating-image json=... />` payload with `dataUrl`. On a thrown request error, surface the error text as a plain message (no SVG fallback).
- Update the tool **description** string to drop SVG/template wording and describe generator selection.

`GeneratedImageCard` (`AssistantChatPanel.tsx`) still accepts `svg` for backward compatibility — leave it; we simply stop producing `svg`.

## 5. Regenerate the nodepod boot snapshot

`web/src/keating/nodepod-boot-files.ts` is **auto-generated** and embeds a compiled copy of `browser-tools`. After the edits run:

```
just generate-nodepod-boot     # = bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts
```

## Verification

- `cd web && bun run typecheck` (or the repo's tsc script) — confirm removed helpers/exports leave no dangling refs.
- `cd web && bun test` — existing browser-tools tests still pass (none reference the deleted SVG builders; verified by grep).
- Manual (`/run` or `bun dev`):
  1. Settings → Image generation: switch generator to **Local**, set a bogus base URL empty → ask the assistant to generate an image → expect the plain "no image generator available" message, **no SVG card**.
  2. Set generator **OpenAI** with no key → same plain message.
  3. With a valid OpenAI key (or a local ComfyUI/LM-style `/images/generations` server) → expect a real raster image card.
- Confirm the model/size/quality choices all originate from `image-generators.ts` (grep for `gpt-image` should only appear in that one module).

/**
 * Browser-compatible Keating tools/commands
 * Uses real implementations from core.ts
 */

import type { AgentTool } from "@earendil-works/pi-agent-core";
import type { KeatingAgentRuntimeConfig } from "./agent-runtime";
import { KeatingStorage, DEFAULT_BROWSER_POLICY } from "./storage";
import { createSpeechTool, type WebSpeechSettings } from "./speech";
import { getProviderApiKey } from "../lib/provider-models";
import { proxiedProviderRequestUrl } from "../lib/provider-proxy";
import {
	buildGoal,
	advanceGoalStep,
	computeGoalProgress,
	type GoalStepInput,
	type GoalStepStatus,
} from "./goals";
import { extractBrowserOutcomes, type BrowserLearnerOutcome } from "./core";

content.text:
All tasks complete. Here's what changed.

## Summary

**1. Central place for image-generator/model choices — new `web/src/lib/image-generators.ts`**
- `IMAGE_GENERATORS` is the single source of truth: each entry defines its endpoint, provider key, model list, sizes, and qualities. `gpt-image-*` now lives *only* here (verified by grep).
- Two backends: **OpenAI** (hosted) and **Local (OpenAI-compatible)** — a local server (ComfyUI/Automatic1111/llama.cpp-style) exposing `/images/generations`, configured by base URL. `localImageEndpoint()` builds the URL with the same `/v1` normalization used elsewhere.

**2. Setting to pick the generator**
- `web/src/keating/ui-settings.ts`: added `imageGenerator`, `imageModel`, `imageSize`, `imageQuality`, `localImageBaseUrl` (with defaults + normalization).
- `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`: new **Image generation** section — generator dropdown, conditional local-endpoint base-URL field, model select/free-text, and size/quality. Switching generator resets model/size/quality.

**3. Templates removed**
- Deleted all SVG builders (`buildLocalImageSvg`, `buildInfographicSvg`, `buildAnatomySvg`, `buildComparisonSvg`, `buildProcessSvg`) and helpers (`wrapSvgText`, `svgTextLines`), their test re-exports, and the obsolete SVG-builder tests.
- `generate_image` now reads the configured generator and calls the generalized `generateImageViaEndpoint`. The `mode=auto/svg` fallback is gone.

**4. No-model result is a plain message**
- If no generator is available (local with no base URL, or OpenAI with no key, or no model configured), the tool returns a short text result pointing to Settings — no image, no placeholder. Request errors also surface as plain text.

**Verification:** `tsc --noEmit` clean, full suite **234 pass / 0 fail**, nodepod boot snapshot regenerated (no dangling refs to removed functions).

The `GeneratedImageCard` renderer still accepts `svg` for backward-compatibility with any old transcripts, but the tool no longer produces it. One thing to note: the local backend treats the model as free-form text (you type the model id your server exposes), since local servers don't share a fixed catalog.

content.text: The previous plan (image-gen) is already implemented. This is a new task, so I'll overwrite. Let me ask the two genuine forks before writing the plan.

questions.question: When the new speech/mic icon in the prompt is clicked, what should it do?

options.label: Adaptive (recommended)
options.description: If a duplex provider (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live) is configured in Speech settings, open a live bidirectional voice conversation; otherwise do push-to-talk STT that transcribes into the prompt box. Matches 'stt or bidirectional if that is configured'.

options.label: STT into prompt only
options.description: Always push-to-talk: record mic, transcribe to text, drop it into the composer for you to review and send. Simpler, no live audio back.

options.label: Bidirectional only
options.description: Always open a live realtime voice session with the tutor (uses existing Realtime/Gemini Live duplex). No plain STT-to-text mode.

questions.question: For the share-link OpenGraph preview image (what shows in Discord/Twitter embeds), which approach?

options.label: Dynamic per-share image
options.description: Render a per-share card server-side at request time (share title baked into the picture, Twitter-card style). Coolest, but adds a runtime image-render route in Nitro. Title + description in the embed text are dynamic either way.

options.label: Branded static image
options.description: Reuse the existing branded og-image.png as the picture, but inject the specific share's title + description into the embed's text/meta. Ships fast, less flashy. You can upgrade to dynamic images later.

You can now continue with these answers in mind.

# Speech-input icon, OG share previews, and image-error fixes

## Context

Follow-up to the image-generation work. Five related changes, grouped into two features and three bug-fixes the user flagged after testing:

**Features**
1. **Speech/mic icon in the prompt composer** — lets the learner talk instead of type. *Adaptive*: if a duplex speech provider (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live) is the configured/enabled provider, open a live bidirectional voice session; otherwise do push-to-talk STT that transcribes into the composer. Auto-detect credentials: a genuine **OpenAI** key (provider `openai`, i.e. real api.openai.com — *not* a custom-base OpenAI-compatible provider) or a **Google/Gemini** key enables it automatically; if both exist, prefer OpenAI.
2. **Dynamic OpenGraph previews for share links** — so `/s/:id` links render rich per-share embeds in Discord/Twitter (title + description + a per-share rendered card image), instead of every link showing the homepage's static card.

**Fixes**
3. `generate_image` **request errors** should surface like every other API error (the classified `ErrorBadge` path), not as a swallowed plain-text result. The *no-generator-configured* guards stay as plain messages (intended), but an actual call failure must propagate.
4. **Insufficient-funds / quota / billing errors** must be classified in the API-error UI (currently unhandled).
5. The **"Re-enter API key"** button must actually recover — store the key under the correct provider and retry the failed turn.

---

## Fixes (do first — small, high-value)

### 3 + 4. Error classification — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Add a `BILLING_ERROR_PATTERNS` regex (e.g. `insufficient.?(funds|quota|credit|balance)|exceeded your current quota|billing|payment required|402|insufficient_quota`) and a new `category: "billing"` branch in `classifyError()` (around `classifyError` at line 507) returning a clear title/description ("Insufficient Credits / Quota", icon e.g. `CircleDollarSign` or existing `CircleAlert`). Place the billing check **before** the generic 429 handling so quota-429s are labelled as billing, not plain rate-limit.
- This automatically covers image-gen failures too, since (fix #3) they now flow through the same assistant-error path.

### 3. `generate_image` request error — `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`
- In the tool body, the `try/catch` around `generateImageViaEndpoint` currently does `return \`Image generation failed: …\``. Change it to **rethrow** (`throw error`) so the failure propagates as a tool/agent error and renders through `ErrorBadge` like other API errors. Keep the *no-endpoint / no-key / no-model* guards returning plain strings (unchanged — that's the intended "no generator available" result).
- Regenerate the nodepod boot snapshot afterward (`bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts`).

### 5. Re-enter API key button — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx` + `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`
Two concrete defects:
- **Wrong provider** when the errored message lacks `provider`: `authErrorFromAgentMessage()` (line 677) falls back to `"unknown"`, so the key gets stored under `unknown` and never fixes the real provider. Fix: thread the active model's provider as the fallback. The converter runs where `agent.state.model.provider` is available (the message-conversion closure near lines 1871/1946) — pass `agent.state.model.provider` as the fallback instead of the literal `"unknown"`.
- **No retry** after a successful key entry: `handleAuthRetry` (line 2907) only calls `onAuthError(provider)` (which just opens the prompt). Make `onAuthError` in `useKeatingAgent.tsx` (line 437), on a successful `promptKeatingApiKey(provider, {force:true})`, **retry the failed turn**: pop the trailing errored assistant message from `agent.state.messages` and call `agent.continue()` (the agent exposes `continue()` — `pi-agent-core/dist/agent.d.ts:107`). Notify via `setLocalVersion`/`onLocalMessagesChanged` so the UI refreshes.

---

## Feature 1 — Speech/mic icon (adaptive)

### Credential detection — extend `web/src/keating/speech.ts`
Add a helper `resolveSpeechCredential(getApiKey)` returning `{ provider: "openai" | "google", apiKey } | null`:
- Try `getApiKey("openai")` first (genuine OpenAI; this naturally excludes custom-base OpenAI-compatible providers, which are stored under their own custom names — satisfying the "distinct from a different base" requirement). Fall back to `getApiKey("google")`. Prefer OpenAI when both exist.
- Use the existing `getProviderApiKey` from `web/src/lib/provider-models.ts` as the `getApiKey` impl (handles OAuth + stored keys).

### Mode decision
Add `speechInputMode(settings: WebSpeechSettings): "duplex" | "stt"`:
- `"duplex"` when speech is enabled **and** the active provider's `kind === "duplex"` (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live). Use `getSpeechProvider(settings.providerId)` / the descriptor `kind` already defined in `speech.ts`.
- otherwise `"stt"`.

### STT (one-shot) — new `web/src/keating/speech-providers/stt.ts`
- Capture mic via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio:true})` + `MediaRecorder` (reuse the mic-permission pattern from `openai-realtime.ts:attachMicrophone`).
- Transcribe:
  - **OpenAI**: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions` (multipart, model `gpt-4o-transcribe` or `whisper-1`) with bearer key. Route through `proxiedProviderRequestUrl` like other OpenAI calls.
  - **Google**: `@google/genai` `generateContent` with inline audio part → transcript text (the package is already used in `gemini-live.ts`).
- Return the transcript string.

### Composer UI — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Add a mic `<button>` in the composer action row beside `ComposerPrimitive.AddAttachment` (around line 2712). Icon: `Mic` (lucide). Render it **only when** `resolveSpeechCredential` returns non-null (compute once on mount / when keys change; cheap async check stored in state).
- Click behavior:
  - `mode === "stt"`: toggle recording (mic icon → recording state with `MicOff`/pulse). On stop, transcribe, then insert text into the composer input (set the composer value; reuse the same mechanism `SuggestedPrompts`/`sendText` uses to populate input, or write to the composer runtime). Let the user review/send — do not auto-send.
  - `mode === "duplex"`: open a lightweight live-session overlay that drives the existing duplex provider with mic enabled (`settings.microphoneEnabled = true`) via `getSpeechProvider(providerId)`; show a "listening / speaking" indicator with a stop button. Reuse `openai-realtime.ts` / `gemini-live.ts` WebRTC/session plumbing; the overlay manages start/stop + `AbortController`.
- Errors from the speech layer reuse the existing `VOICE_ERROR_PATTERNS` classification so they show as non-fatal "Voice Model Error".

---

## Feature 2 — Dynamic OG previews for share links

Crawlers (Discord/Twitter) don't run the SPA JS, so `/s/:id` currently serves the static homepage `index.html` meta. Add server-side meta + image rendering in the Nitro server (`web/server/`, `web/nitro.config.ts`).

### a) Per-share HTML with injected meta — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].ts`
- Register a route `"/s/**" → server/routes/s/[id].ts` in `nitro.config.ts` `handlers` (takes precedence over the `"/**": { static: true }` SPA fallback).
- Read the share via `useStorage("keating:share").getItem(id)` (same store written by `server/api/share/index.ts`). Pull `title`, derive a `description` from the first user/assistant message text (truncated, sanitized).
- Read the built SPA shell (`dist/index.html`) — via Nitro public-asset storage or `fs.readFile`. Replace/append the OG + Twitter meta tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:url` = the share URL, `og:image` = `/s/:id/og.png`, `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:title/description/image`) and `<title>`. Return the HTML (`text/html`).
- If the share is missing, fall through to the normal SPA shell (so the client renders its own 404). Escape all interpolated text to avoid meta-tag injection.

### b) Per-share image — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].og.png.ts`
- Render with **satori** (already in `node_modules/satori`) → SVG, then **@resvg/resvg-wasm** (`node_modules/@resvg/resvg-wasm`) → PNG (1200×630). Mirror the visual language of `web/src/og-image.tsx` (dark terminal card, accent colors) but inject the share's `title` + a short snippet.
- Satori needs a font buffer: add one TTF under `web/server/assets/fonts/` (e.g. Inter/DejaVu Sans) and load it via Nitro server assets / `fs`. resvg-wasm needs its wasm initialized once (cache the module + font in module scope).
- Cache the rendered PNG per id in `useStorage("keating:share-og")` (or an in-memory LRU) to avoid re-rendering on every crawl. Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=...` and `Content-Type: image/png`.
- Add matching `routeRules`/headers if needed so the `.png` isn't caught by the SPA fallback.

### c) Share URL
- Confirm `SharedSession` route is `/s/$shareId` (`web/src/App.tsx:73`). The OG `og:url`/canonical should point at the absolute `https://<host>/s/:id`. Derive host from the request (`getRequestURL(event)`).

---

## Verification

- `cd web && bun run typecheck` and `bun test` (existing suites: error classification, ui-settings, speech). Add focused unit tests: `classifyError` billing case; `resolveSpeechCredential` precedence (OpenAI over Google; ignores absence); `speechInputMode` mapping.
- **Fix #3/#4**: with a configured generator that returns 402/insufficient_quota, `generate_image` shows the classified billing `ErrorBadge` (not plain text); the no-key case still shows the plain "no image generator available" message.
- **Fix #5**: trigger a 401 from a provider, click *Re-enter API key*, enter a valid key → the failed turn re-runs and completes; verify the key is stored under the real provider (not `unknown`).
- **Speech**: with only a Google key, mic icon appears and STT fills the composer; with OpenAI Realtime selected + key, the icon opens a live session; with no speech credential, the icon is hidden.
- **OG**: `curl -A "Discordbot" https://<host>/s/<id>` returns HTML whose `og:title`/`og:description` match the share; open `/s/<id>/og.png` and confirm a per-share card renders; validate the embed in a Discord message / a card validator.
- Run `bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts` after editing `browser-tools.ts`.

+Follow-up to the image-generation work. Five related changes, grouped into two features and three bug-fixes the user flagged after testing:

-- **Hardcodes** OpenAI as the only real image backend (`gpt-image-1.5`, endpoint `api.openai.com/v1/images/generations`) inside `generateOpenAiImage`. There is no setting to choose a generator, and the model id lives inline in the tool rather than in a central place.

-- **Falls back to hand-built SVG "templates"** (`buildLocalImageSvg` → `buildInfographicSvg` / `buildAnatomySvg` / `buildComparisonSvg` / `buildProcessSvg`) whenever no key is configured or `mode` is `auto`/`svg`. The user considers these template SVGs useless.

+1. **Speech/mic icon in the prompt composer** — lets the learner talk instead of type. *Adaptive*: if a duplex speech provider (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live) is the configured/enabled provider, open a live bidirectional voice session; otherwise do push-to-talk STT that transcribes into the composer. +- Try `getApiKey("openai")` first (genuine OpenAI; this naturally excludes custom-base OpenAI-compatible providers, which are stored under their own custom names — satisfying the "distinct from a different base" requirement). Fall back to `getApiKey("google")`. Prefer OpenAI when both exist.
+- Use the existing `getProviderApiKey` from `web/src/lib/provider-models.ts` as the `getApiKey` impl (handles OAuth + stored keys).

+- `"duplex"` when speech is enabled **and** the active provider's `kind === "duplex"` (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live). Use `getSpeechProvider(settings.providerId)` / the descriptor `kind` already defined in `speech.ts`.
+- Capture mic via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio:true})` + `MediaRecorder` (reuse the mic-permission pattern from `openai-realtime.ts:attachMicrophone`).

+  - **OpenAI**: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions` (multipart, model `gpt-4o-transcribe` or `whisper-1`) with bearer key. Route through `proxiedProviderRequestUrl` like other OpenAI calls.
+  - **Google**: `@google/genai` `generateContent` with inline audio part → transcript text (the package is already used in `gemini-live.ts`).
-- `imageModel: string` (default `""` → tool resolves to generator's first model)

+- Add a mic `<button>` in the composer action row beside `ComposerPrimitive.AddAttachment` (around line 2712). Icon: `Mic` (lucide). Render it **only when** `resolveSpeechCredential` returns non-null (compute once on mount / when keys change; cheap async check stored in state).

+  - `mode === "stt"`: toggle recording (mic icon → recording state with `MicOff`/pulse). On stop, transcribe, then insert text into the composer input (set the composer value; reuse the same mechanism `SuggestedPrompts`/`sendText` uses to populate input, or write to the composer runtime). Let the user review/send — do not auto-send.

+  - `mode === "duplex"`: open a lightweight live-session overlay that drives the existing duplex provider with mic enabled (`settings.microphoneEnabled = true`) via `getSpeechProvider(providerId)`; show a "listening / speaking" indicator with a stop button. Reuse `openai-realtime.ts` / `gemini-live.ts` WebRTC/session plumbing; the overlay manages start/stop + `AbortController`.
+- Errors from the speech layer reuse the existing `VOICE_ERROR_PATTERNS` classification so they show as non-fatal "Voice Model Error".

-Normalize `imageGenerator` against `IMAGE_GENERATORS` ids; coerce the rest to strings. No new event plumbing needed — existing `saveKeatingUiSettings` / `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` already broadcast.
+## Feature 2 — Dynamic OG previews for share links
-- Select for **model** (options = selected generator's `models`; for `local` with empty models, a free-text input).
-- Text input for **Local endpoint base URL**, shown only when `imageGenerator === "local"` (e.g. `http://localhost:1234`).

+Crawlers (Discord/Twitter) don't run the SPA JS, so `/s/:id` currently serves the static homepage `index.html` meta. Add server-side meta + image rendering in the Nitro server (`web/server/`, `web/nitro.config.ts`).

-Follow the existing controlled-select pattern in this tab (it already renders selects bound to `loadKeatingUiSettings()` and calls `saveKeatingUiSettings`). The local API key (if the endpoint needs one) is entered through the existing Providers key storage under name `local-image` via `getProviderApiKey`/`providerKeys`; note this in the field helper text. (If a key field is desired inline, reuse `getAppStorage().providerKeys.set("local-image", ...)`.)
+### a) Per-share HTML with injected meta — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].ts`
+- Register a route `"/s/**" → server/routes/s/[id].ts` in `nitro.config.ts` `handlers` (takes precedence over the `"/**": { static: true }` SPA fallback).

+- Read the share via `useStorage("keating:share").getItem(id)` (same store written by `server/api/share/index.ts`). Pull `title`, derive a `description` from the first user/assistant message text (truncated, sanitized).

+- Read the built SPA shell (`dist/index.html`) — via Nitro public-asset storage or `fs.readFile`. Replace/append the OG + Twitter meta tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:url` = the share URL, `og:image` = `/s/:id/og.png`, `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:title/description/image`) and `<title>`. Return the HTML (`text/html`).
+- If the share is missing, fall through to the normal SPA shell (so the client renders its own 404). Escape all interpolated text to avoid meta-tag injection.

+- Render with **satori** (already in `node_modules/satori`) → SVG, then **@resvg/resvg-wasm** (`node_modules/@resvg/resvg-wasm`) → PNG (1200×630). Mirror the visual language of `web/src/og-image.tsx` (dark terminal card, accent colors) but inject the share's `title` + a short snippet.

+- Satori needs a font buffer: add one TTF under `web/server/assets/fonts/` (e.g. Inter/DejaVu Sans) and load it via Nitro server assets / `fs`. resvg-wasm needs its wasm initialized once (cache the module + font in module scope).

+- Cache the rendered PNG per id in `useStorage("keating:share-og")` (or an in-memory LRU) to avoid re-rendering on every crawl. Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=...` and `Content-Type: image/png`.
+- Add matching `routeRules`/headers if needed so the `.png` isn't caught by the SPA fallback.
-**Delete** (and their only-internal helpers, confirmed unused elsewhere except test re-exports):
-- The test re-exports `__test_buildProcessSvg`, `__test_buildLocalImageSvg`, `__test_buildInfographicSvg` (bottom of file, ~line 2190). Grep confirms no test file references them.

+- Confirm `SharedSession` route is `/s/$shareId` (`web/src/App.tsx:73`). The OG `og:url`/canonical should point at the absolute `https://<host>/s/:id`. Derive host from the request (`getRequestURL(event)`).

-**Generalize** `generateOpenAiImage` → `generateImageViaEndpoint({ endpoint, apiKey, model, size, quality, prompt })`: same fetch/proxy logic (`proxiedProviderRequestUrl`, bearer header, `b64_json` extraction), but endpoint + key are passed in instead of hardcoded.
-- Keep the authored-content guard (reject generic title/subtitle/<3 points) — still desirable.
-- Drop the `mode` (`auto`/`svg`/`model`) param and all SVG branches. Keep `kind`/`labels`/`points` only as prompt enrichment.
-  - `local`: build endpoint from `settings.localImageBaseUrl`; key = `getProviderApiKey("local-image")` (may be empty for keyless local servers).

-  - openai with no key, OR local with no base URL → e.g. `"No image generation model is available. Choose and configure one in Settings → Image generation (the local endpoint needs a base URL; OpenAI needs an API key)."`

-- Otherwise call `generateImageViaEndpoint`, return the existing `<keating-image json=... />` payload with `dataUrl`. On a thrown request error, surface the error text as a plain message (no SVG fallback).
-- Update the tool **description** string to drop SVG/template wording and describe generator selection.
-`GeneratedImageCard` (`AssistantChatPanel.tsx`) still accepts `svg` for backward compatibility — leave it; we simply stop producing `svg`.
-`web/src/keating/nodepod-boot-files.ts` is **auto-generated** and embeds a compiled copy of `browser-tools`. After the edits run:
-- `cd web && bun run typecheck` (or the repo's tsc script) — confirm removed helpers/exports leave no dangling refs.
-- `cd web && bun test` — existing browser-tools tests still pass (none reference the deleted SVG builders; verified by grep).

-  1. Settings → Image generation: switch generator to **Local**, set a bogus base URL empty → ask the assistant to generate an image → expect the plain "no image generator available" message, **no SVG card**.
-  2. Set generator **OpenAI** with no key → same plain message.
-  3. With a valid OpenAI key (or a local ComfyUI/LM-style `/images/generations` server) → expect a real raster image card.
-- Confirm the model/size/quality choices all originate from `image-generators.ts` (grep for `gpt-image` should only appear in that one module).

+- `cd web && bun run typecheck` and `bun test` (existing suites: error classification, ui-settings, speech). Add focused unit tests: `classifyError` billing case; `resolveSpeechCredential` precedence (OpenAI over Google; ignores absence); `speechInputMode` mapping.

+- **Fix #3/#4**: with a configured generator that returns 402/insufficient_quota, `generate_image` shows the classified billing `ErrorBadge` (not plain text); the no-key case still shows the plain "no image generator available" message.
+- **Fix #5**: trigger a 401 from a provider, click *Re-enter API key*, enter a valid key → the failed turn re-runs and completes; verify the key is stored under the real provider (not `unknown`).
+- **Speech**: with only a Google key, mic icon appears and STT fills the composer; with OpenAI Realtime selected + key, the icon opens a live session; with no speech credential, the icon is hidden.

+- **OG**: `curl -A "Discordbot" https://<host>/s/<id>` returns HTML whose `og:title`/`og:description` match the share; open `/s/<id>/og.png` and confirm a per-share card renders; validate the embed in a Discord message / a card validator.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Fix the generate-image flow

## Context

The `generate_image` browser tool (`web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`) currently:

- **Hardcodes** OpenAI as the only real image backend (`gpt-image-1.5`, endpoint `api.openai.com/v1/images/generations`) inside `generateOpenAiImage`. There is no setting to choose a generator, and the model id lives inline in the tool rather than in a central place.
- **Falls back to hand-built SVG "templates"** (`buildLocalImageSvg` → `buildInfographicSvg` / `buildAnatomySvg` / `buildComparisonSvg` / `buildProcessSvg`) whenever no key is configured or `mode` is `auto`/`svg`. The user considers these template SVGs useless.

Goal:
1. Add a setting to pick the image generator — **local (a local OpenAI‑compatible endpoint)** or **remote (OpenAI)** — with all model choices defined in one central module.
2. Remove the SVG template generators entirely. When no image generator is available/configured, `generate_image` returns a **plain message** (no image, no placeholder).

Decisions confirmed with user:
- "Local" = a configurable **local OpenAI‑compatible** `/images/generations` endpoint (reuses existing proxy plumbing).
- No-model case = **plain text result**, no SVG.

## 1. Central image-generator config — new file `web/src/lib/image-generators.ts`

Single source of truth for image backends + their model choices. Mirrors the style of `provider-models.ts`.

```ts
export type ImageGeneratorId = "openai" | "local";

export interface ImageGeneratorOption {
  id: ImageGeneratorId;
  label: string;
  description: string;
  // "openai": fixed remote endpoint + provider key "openai"
  // "local":  base URL comes from settings.localImageBaseUrl
  fixedEndpoint?: string;       // openai -> https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations
  providerKey?: string;         // provider name for getProviderApiKey()  (openai -> "openai", local -> "local-image")
  models: string[];             // first entry = default
  sizes: string[];              // first = default
  qualities: string[];          // first = default
}

export const IMAGE_GENERATORS: ImageGeneratorOption[] = [
  { id: "openai", label: "OpenAI", ..., fixedEndpoint: ".../v1/images/generations", providerKey: "openai",
    models: ["gpt-image-1.5", "gpt-image-1", "gpt-image-1-mini"], sizes: ["1024x1024","1536x1024","1024x1536"], qualities: ["medium","low","high"] },
  { id: "local", label: "Local (OpenAI-compatible)", ..., providerKey: "local-image",
    models: [], sizes: [...], qualities: [...] },  // models discovered/free-form; default model from settings.imageModel
];

export function getImageGenerator(id: string): ImageGeneratorOption | undefined { ... }
export const DEFAULT_IMAGE_GENERATOR_ID: ImageGeneratorId = "openai";
```

The `local` endpoint is built from the base URL: `trimTrailingSlash(baseUrl)` + (`/v1/images/generations` or `/images/generations` if base already ends in `/v1`). Reuse the trailing-slash + `/v1` normalization logic already present in `provider-models.ts` (`trimTrailingSlash`, `openAiCompatibleBaseUrls` pattern).

## 2. Settings — `web/src/keating/ui-settings.ts`

Add to `KeatingUiSettings`, `DEFAULT_UI_SETTINGS`, and `normalizeSettings()`:

- `imageGenerator: ImageGeneratorId` (default `"openai"`)
- `imageModel: string` (default `""` → tool resolves to generator's first model)
- `imageSize: string` (default `""`)
- `imageQuality: string` (default `""`)
- `localImageBaseUrl: string` (default `""`)

Normalize `imageGenerator` against `IMAGE_GENERATORS` ids; coerce the rest to strings. No new event plumbing needed — existing `saveKeatingUiSettings` / `subscribeKeatingUiSettings` already broadcast.

## 3. Settings UI — `web/src/components/KeatingUiSettingsTab.tsx`

Add an "Image generation" section:
- Select for **generator** (maps over `IMAGE_GENERATORS`).
- Select for **model** (options = selected generator's `models`; for `local` with empty models, a free-text input).
- Text input for **Local endpoint base URL**, shown only when `imageGenerator === "local"` (e.g. `http://localhost:1234`).
- Optional selects for size / quality.

Follow the existing controlled-select pattern in this tab (it already renders selects bound to `loadKeatingUiSettings()` and calls `saveKeatingUiSettings`). The local API key (if the endpoint needs one) is entered through the existing Providers key storage under name `local-image` via `getProviderApiKey`/`providerKeys`; note this in the field helper text. (If a key field is desired inline, reuse `getAppStorage().providerKeys.set("local-image", ...)`.)

## 4. Rework the tool — `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`

**Delete** (and their only-internal helpers, confirmed unused elsewhere except test re-exports):
- `wrapSvgText`, `svgTextLines`
- `buildInfographicSvg`, `buildAnatomySvg`, `buildComparisonSvg`, `buildProcessSvg`, `buildLocalImageSvg`
- The test re-exports `__test_buildProcessSvg`, `__test_buildLocalImageSvg`, `__test_buildInfographicSvg` (bottom of file, ~line 2190). Grep confirms no test file references them.

**Generalize** `generateOpenAiImage` → `generateImageViaEndpoint({ endpoint, apiKey, model, size, quality, prompt })`: same fetch/proxy logic (`proxiedProviderRequestUrl`, bearer header, `b64_json` extraction), but endpoint + key are passed in instead of hardcoded.

**Rewrite the `generate_image` tool body** (~lines 1106-1198):
- Keep the authored-content guard (reject generic title/subtitle/<3 points) — still desirable.
- Drop the `mode` (`auto`/`svg`/`model`) param and all SVG branches. Keep `kind`/`labels`/`points` only as prompt enrichment.
- Resolve generator from settings: `const gen = getImageGenerator(loadKeatingUiSettings().imageGenerator)`.
- Resolve endpoint + apiKey:
  - `openai`: `gen.fixedEndpoint`, key = `getProviderApiKey("openai")`.
  - `local`: build endpoint from `settings.localImageBaseUrl`; key = `getProviderApiKey("local-image")` (may be empty for keyless local servers).
- **No-model guard** → return a plain string (no `<keating-image>`):
  - openai with no key, OR local with no base URL → e.g. `"No image generation model is available. Choose and configure one in Settings → Image generation (the local endpoint needs a base URL; OpenAI needs an API key)."`
- Otherwise call `generateImageViaEndpoint`, return the existing `<keating-image json=... />` payload with `dataUrl`. On a thrown request error, surface the error text as a plain message (no SVG fallback).
- Update the tool **description** string to drop SVG/template wording and describe generator selection.

`GeneratedImageCard` (`AssistantChatPanel.tsx`) still accepts `svg` for backward compatibility — leave it; we simply stop producing `svg`.

## 5. Regenerate the nodepod boot snapshot

`web/src/keating/nodepod-boot-files.ts` is **auto-generated** and embeds a compiled copy of `browser-tools`. After the edits run:

```
just generate-nodepod-boot     # = bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts
```

## Verification

- `cd web && bun run typecheck` (or the repo's tsc script) — confirm removed helpers/exports leave no dangling refs.
- `cd web && bun test` — existing browser-tools tests still pass (none reference the deleted SVG builders; verified by grep).
- Manual (`/run` or `bun dev`):
  1. Settings → Image generation: switch generator to **Local**, set a bogus base URL empty → ask the assistant to generate an image → expect the plain "no image generator available" message, **no SVG card**.
  2. Set generator **OpenAI** with no key → same plain message.
  3. With a valid OpenAI key (or a local ComfyUI/LM-style `/images/generations` server) → expect a real raster image card.
- Confirm the model/size/quality choices all originate from `image-generators.ts` (grep for `gpt-image` should only appear in that one module).

content.text: Confirmed the root cause. The PWA service worker (`VitePWA`, `generateSW`) has **no `navigateFallbackDenylist`**, so `navigateFallback: 'index.html'` lets the SW serve a stale/precached SPA shell for `/s/:id`. When that cached shell references an asset hash the current server no longer has, the asset request falls through and gets HTML back → the exact "module … MIME type text/html" error. It surfaces on share links because those are opened cold in clients an old SW still controls. This fix is also a prerequisite for the OG work (the SW must let `/s/:id` reach the server). Let me fold it into the plan as the first, urgent fix.

## Fixes (do first — small, high-value)

### 0. Share link is broken: "module script … MIME type text/html" — **URGENT**, do first
Root cause: the PWA service worker. `VitePWA` (`web/vite.config.ts`, ~line 394) uses `generateSW` with `navigateFallback: 'index.html'` (the default) and **no `navigateFallbackDenylist`**. Opening a share link cold in a client still controlled by an older service worker serves the *precached* SPA shell, which references stale `/assets/index-<oldhash>.js`; that request misses and resolves to HTML, producing the module-MIME error. It shows on share links because those are the routes opened in clients an old SW controls.

Fix in `web/vite.config.ts` `workbox`:
- Add `navigateFallbackDenylist: [/^\/s\//, /^\/api\//, /^\/assets\//]` so the SW never substitutes the SPA shell for share routes, API routes, or hashed assets — `/s/:id` navigations and asset requests always hit the server with correct content-types.
- This is also a **prerequisite for Feature 2**: the server-rendered `/s/:id` HTML (with OG meta) must not be shadowed by the precached shell.
- The Nitro side is already safe (`nitro.config.ts` has `"/assets/**": { fallthrough: false }`, so missing assets 404 instead of returning index.html). The defect is purely the SW fallback.
- Verify: register the SW, deploy a new build, open an old `/s/:id` in a controlled client → loads cleanly; module requests never return `text/html`.

### 3 + 4. Error classification

input.plan:
# Speech-input icon, OG share previews, and image-error fixes

## Context

Follow-up to the image-generation work. Five related changes, grouped into two features and three bug-fixes the user flagged after testing:

**Features**
1. **Speech/mic icon in the prompt composer** — lets the learner talk instead of type. *Adaptive*: if a duplex speech provider (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live) is the configured/enabled provider, open a live bidirectional voice session; otherwise do push-to-talk STT that transcribes into the composer. Auto-detect credentials: a genuine **OpenAI** key (provider `openai`, i.e. real api.openai.com — *not* a custom-base OpenAI-compatible provider) or a **Google/Gemini** key enables it automatically; if both exist, prefer OpenAI.
2. **Dynamic OpenGraph previews for share links** — so `/s/:id` links render rich per-share embeds in Discord/Twitter (title + description + a per-share rendered card image), instead of every link showing the homepage's static card.

**Fixes**
3. `generate_image` **request errors** should surface like every other API error (the classified `ErrorBadge` path), not as a swallowed plain-text result. The *no-generator-configured* guards stay as plain messages (intended), but an actual call failure must propagate.
4. **Insufficient-funds / quota / billing errors** must be classified in the API-error UI (currently unhandled).
5. The **"Re-enter API key"** button must actually recover — store the key under the correct provider and retry the failed turn.

---

## Fixes (do first — small, high-value)

### 0. Share link is broken: "module script … MIME type text/html" — **URGENT**, do first
Root cause: the PWA service worker. `VitePWA` (`web/vite.config.ts`, ~line 394) uses `generateSW` with `navigateFallback: 'index.html'` (the default) and **no `navigateFallbackDenylist`**. Opening a share link cold in a client still controlled by an older service worker serves the *precached* SPA shell, which references stale `/assets/index-<oldhash>.js`; that request misses and resolves to HTML, producing the module-MIME error. It shows on share links because those are the routes opened in clients an old SW controls.

Fix in `web/vite.config.ts` `workbox`:
- Add `navigateFallbackDenylist: [/^\/s\//, /^\/api\//, /^\/assets\//]` so the SW never substitutes the SPA shell for share routes, API routes, or hashed assets — `/s/:id` navigations and asset requests always hit the server with correct content-types.
- This is also a **prerequisite for Feature 2**: the server-rendered `/s/:id` HTML (with OG meta) must not be shadowed by the precached shell.
- The Nitro side is already safe (`nitro.config.ts` has `"/assets/**": { fallthrough: false }`, so missing assets 404 instead of returning index.html). The defect is purely the SW fallback.
- Verify: register the SW, deploy a new build, open an old `/s/:id` in a controlled client → loads cleanly; module requests never return `text/html`.

### 3 + 4. Error classification — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Add a `BILLING_ERROR_PATTERNS` regex (e.g. `insufficient.?(funds|quota|credit|balance)|exceeded your current quota|billing|payment required|402|insufficient_quota`) and a new `category: "billing"` branch in `classifyError()` (around `classifyError` at line 507) returning a clear title/description ("Insufficient Credits / Quota", icon e.g. `CircleDollarSign` or existing `CircleAlert`). Place the billing check **before** the generic 429 handling so quota-429s are labelled as billing, not plain rate-limit.
- This automatically covers image-gen failures too, since (fix #3) they now flow through the same assistant-error path.

### 3. `generate_image` request error — `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`
- In the tool body, the `try/catch` around `generateImageViaEndpoint` currently does `return \`Image generation failed: …\``. Change it to **rethrow** (`throw error`) so the failure propagates as a tool/agent error and renders through `ErrorBadge` like other API errors. Keep the *no-endpoint / no-key / no-model* guards returning plain strings (unchanged — that's the intended "no generator available" result).
- Regenerate the nodepod boot snapshot afterward (`bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts`).

### 5. Re-enter API key button — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx` + `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`
Two concrete defects:
- **Wrong provider** when the errored message lacks `provider`: `authErrorFromAgentMessage()` (line 677) falls back to `"unknown"`, so the key gets stored under `unknown` and never fixes the real provider. Fix: thread the active model's provider as the fallback. The converter runs where `agent.state.model.provider` is available (the message-conversion closure near lines 1871/1946) — pass `agent.state.model.provider` as the fallback instead of the literal `"unknown"`.
- **No retry** after a successful key entry: `handleAuthRetry` (line 2907) only calls `onAuthError(provider)` (which just opens the prompt). Make `onAuthError` in `useKeatingAgent.tsx` (line 437), on a successful `promptKeatingApiKey(provider, {force:true})`, **retry the failed turn**: pop the trailing errored assistant message from `agent.state.messages` and call `agent.continue()` (the agent exposes `continue()` — `pi-agent-core/dist/agent.d.ts:107`). Notify via `setLocalVersion`/`onLocalMessagesChanged` so the UI refreshes.

---

## Feature 1 — Speech/mic icon (adaptive)

### Credential detection — extend `web/src/keating/speech.ts`
Add a helper `resolveSpeechCredential(getApiKey)` returning `{ provider: "openai" | "google", apiKey } | null`:
- Try `getApiKey("openai")` first (genuine OpenAI; this naturally excludes custom-base OpenAI-compatible providers, which are stored under their own custom names — satisfying the "distinct from a different base" requirement). Fall back to `getApiKey("google")`. Prefer OpenAI when both exist.
- Use the existing `getProviderApiKey` from `web/src/lib/provider-models.ts` as the `getApiKey` impl (handles OAuth + stored keys).

### Mode decision
Add `speechInputMode(settings: WebSpeechSettings): "duplex" | "stt"`:
- `"duplex"` when speech is enabled **and** the active provider's `kind === "duplex"` (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live). Use `getSpeechProvider(settings.providerId)` / the descriptor `kind` already defined in `speech.ts`.
- otherwise `"stt"`.

### STT (one-shot) — new `web/src/keating/speech-providers/stt.ts`
- Capture mic via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio:true})` + `MediaRecorder` (reuse the mic-permission pattern from `openai-realtime.ts:attachMicrophone`).
- Transcribe:
  - **OpenAI**: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions` (multipart, model `gpt-4o-transcribe` or `whisper-1`) with bearer key. Route through `proxiedProviderRequestUrl` like other OpenAI calls.
  - **Google**: `@google/genai` `generateContent` with inline audio part → transcript text (the package is already used in `gemini-live.ts`).
- Return the transcript string.

### Composer UI — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Add a mic `<button>` in the composer action row beside `ComposerPrimitive.AddAttachment` (around line 2712). Icon: `Mic` (lucide). Render it **only when** `resolveSpeechCredential` returns non-null (compute once on mount / when keys change; cheap async check stored in state).
- Click behavior:
  - `mode === "stt"`: toggle recording (mic icon → recording state with `MicOff`/pulse). On stop, transcribe, then insert text into the composer input (set the composer value; reuse the same mechanism `SuggestedPrompts`/`sendText` uses to populate input, or write to the composer runtime). Let the user review/send — do not auto-send.
  - `mode === "duplex"`: open a lightweight live-session overlay that drives the existing duplex provider with mic enabled (`settings.microphoneEnabled = true`) via `getSpeechProvider(providerId)`; show a "listening / speaking" indicator with a stop button. Reuse `openai-realtime.ts` / `gemini-live.ts` WebRTC/session plumbing; the overlay manages start/stop + `AbortController`.
- Errors from the speech layer reuse the existing `VOICE_ERROR_PATTERNS` classification so they show as non-fatal "Voice Model Error".

---

## Feature 2 — Dynamic OG previews for share links

Crawlers (Discord/Twitter) don't run the SPA JS, so `/s/:id` currently serves the static homepage `index.html` meta. Add server-side meta + image rendering in the Nitro server (`web/server/`, `web/nitro.config.ts`).

### a) Per-share HTML with injected meta — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].ts`
- Register a route `"/s/**" → server/routes/s/[id].ts` in `nitro.config.ts` `handlers` (takes precedence over the `"/**": { static: true }` SPA fallback).
- Read the share via `useStorage("keating:share").getItem(id)` (same store written by `server/api/share/index.ts`). Pull `title`, derive a `description` from the first user/assistant message text (truncated, sanitized).
- Read the built SPA shell (`dist/index.html`) — via Nitro public-asset storage or `fs.readFile`. Replace/append the OG + Twitter meta tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:url` = the share URL, `og:image` = `/s/:id/og.png`, `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:title/description/image`) and `<title>`. Return the HTML (`text/html`).
- If the share is missing, fall through to the normal SPA shell (so the client renders its own 404). Escape all interpolated text to avoid meta-tag injection.

### b) Per-share image — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].og.png.ts`
- Render with **satori** (already in `node_modules/satori`) → SVG, then **@resvg/resvg-wasm** (`node_modules/@resvg/resvg-wasm`) → PNG (1200×630). Mirror the visual language of `web/src/og-image.tsx` (dark terminal card, accent colors) but inject the share's `title` + a short snippet.
- Satori needs a font buffer: add one TTF under `web/server/assets/fonts/` (e.g. Inter/DejaVu Sans) and load it via Nitro server assets / `fs`. resvg-wasm needs its wasm initialized once (cache the module + font in module scope).
- Cache the rendered PNG per id in `useStorage("keating:share-og")` (or an in-memory LRU) to avoid re-rendering on every crawl. Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=...` and `Content-Type: image/png`.
- Add matching `routeRules`/headers if needed so the `.png` isn't caught by the SPA fallback.

### c) Share URL
- Confirm `SharedSession` route is `/s/$shareId` (`web/src/App.tsx:73`). The OG `og:url`/canonical should point at the absolute `https://<host>/s/:id`. Derive host from the request (`getRequestURL(event)`).

---

## Verification

- `cd web && bun run typecheck` and `bun test` (existing suites: error classification, ui-settings, speech). Add focused unit tests: `classifyError` billing case; `resolveSpeechCredential` precedence (OpenAI over Google; ignores absence); `speechInputMode` mapping.
- **Fix #3/#4**: with a configured generator that returns 402/insufficient_quota, `generate_image` shows the classified billing `ErrorBadge` (not plain text); the no-key case still shows the plain "no image generator available" message.
- **Fix #5**: trigger a 401 from a provider, click *Re-enter API key*, enter a valid key → the failed turn re-runs and completes; verify the key is stored under the real provider (not `unknown`).
- **Speech**: with only a Google key, mic icon appears and STT fills the composer; with OpenAI Realtime selected + key, the icon opens a live session; with no speech credential, the icon is hidden.
- **OG**: `curl -A "Discordbot" https://<host>/s/<id>` returns HTML whose `og:title`/`og:description` match the share; open `/s/<id>/og.png` and confirm a per-share card renders; validate the embed in a Discord message / a card validator.
- Run `bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts` after editing `browser-tools.ts`.

- Add a `BILLING_ERROR_PATTERNS` regex (e.g. `insufficient.?(funds|quota|credit|balance)|exceeded your current quota|billing|payment required|402|insufficient_quota`) and a new `category: "billing"` branch in `classifyError()` (around `classifyError` at line 507) returning a clear title/description ("Insufficient Credits / Quota", icon e.g. `CircleDollarSign` or existing `CircleAlert`). Place the billing check **before** the generic 429 handling so quota-429s are labelled as billing, not plain rate-limit.
- This automatically covers image-gen failures too, since (fix #3) they now flow through the same assistant-error path.

### 3. `generate_image` request error — `web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`
- In the tool body, the `try/catch` around `generateImageViaEndpoint` currently does `return \`Image generation failed: …\``. Change it to **rethrow** (`throw error`) so the failure propagates as a tool/agent error and renders through `ErrorBadge` like other API errors. Keep the *no-endpoint / no-key / no-model* guards returning plain strings (unchanged — that's the intended "no generator available" result).
- Regenerate the nodepod boot snapshot afterward (`bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts`).

### 5. Re-enter API key button — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx` + `web/src/hooks/useKeatingAgent.tsx`
Two concrete defects:
- **Wrong provider** when the errored message lacks `provider`: `authErrorFromAgentMessage()` (line 677) falls back to `"unknown"`, so the key gets stored under `unknown` and never fixes the real provider. Fix: thread the active model's provider as the fallback. The converter runs where `agent.state.model.provider` is available (the message-conversion closure near lines 1871/1946) — pass `agent.state.model.provider` as the fallback instead of the literal `"unknown"`.
- **No retry** after a successful key entry: `handleAuthRetry` (line 2907) only calls `onAuthError(provider)` (which just opens the prompt). Make `onAuthError` in `useKeatingAgent.tsx` (line 437), on a successful `promptKeatingApiKey(provider, {force:true})`, **retry the failed turn**: pop the trailing errored assistant message from `agent.state.messages` and call `agent.continue()` (the agent exposes `continue()` — `pi-agent-core/dist/agent.d.ts:107`). Notify via `setLocalVersion`/`onLocalMessagesChanged` so the UI refreshes.

---

## Feature 1 — Speech/mic icon (adaptive)

### Credential detection — extend `web/src/keating/speech.ts`
Add a helper `resolveSpeechCredential(getApiKey)` returning `{ provider: "openai" | "google", apiKey } | null`:
- Try `getApiKey("openai")` first (genuine OpenAI; this naturally excludes custom-base OpenAI-compatible providers, which are stored under their own custom names — satisfying the "distinct from a different base" requirement). Fall back to `getApiKey("google")`. Prefer OpenAI when both exist.
- Use the existing `getProviderApiKey` from `web/src/lib/provider-models.ts` as the `getApiKey` impl (handles OAuth + stored keys).

### Mode decision
Add `speechInputMode(settings: WebSpeechSettings): "duplex" | "stt"`:
- `"duplex"` when speech is enabled **and** the active provider's `kind === "duplex"` (OpenAI Realtime / Gemini Live). Use `getSpeechProvider(settings.providerId)` / the descriptor `kind` already defined in `speech.ts`.
- otherwise `"stt"`.

### STT (one-shot) — new `web/src/keating/speech-providers/stt.ts`
- Capture mic via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio:true})` + `MediaRecorder` (reuse the mic-permission pattern from `openai-realtime.ts:attachMicrophone`).
- Transcribe:
  - **OpenAI**: `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions` (multipart, model `gpt-4o-transcribe` or `whisper-1`) with bearer key. Route through `proxiedProviderRequestUrl` like other OpenAI calls.
  - **Google**: `@google/genai` `generateContent` with inline audio part → transcript text (the package is already used in `gemini-live.ts`).
- Return the transcript string.

### Composer UI — `web/src/components/AssistantChatPanel.tsx`
- Add a mic `<button>` in the composer action row beside `ComposerPrimitive.AddAttachment` (around line 2712). Icon: `Mic` (lucide). Render it **only when** `resolveSpeechCredential` returns non-null (compute once on mount / when keys change; cheap async check stored in state).
- Click behavior:
  - `mode === "stt"`: toggle recording (mic icon → recording state with `MicOff`/pulse). On stop, transcribe, then insert text into the composer input (set the composer value; reuse the same mechanism `SuggestedPrompts`/`sendText` uses to populate input, or write to the composer runtime). Let the user review/send — do not auto-send.
  - `mode === "duplex"`: open a lightweight live-session overlay that drives the existing duplex provider with mic enabled (`settings.microphoneEnabled = true`) via `getSpeechProvider(providerId)`; show a "listening / speaking" indicator with a stop button. Reuse `openai-realtime.ts` / `gemini-live.ts` WebRTC/session plumbing; the overlay manages start/stop + `AbortController`.
- Errors from the speech layer reuse the existing `VOICE_ERROR_PATTERNS` classification so they show as non-fatal "Voice Model Error".

---

## Feature 2 — Dynamic OG previews for share links

Crawlers (Discord/Twitter) don't run the SPA JS, so `/s/:id` currently serves the static homepage `index.html` meta. Add server-side meta + image rendering in the Nitro server (`web/server/`, `web/nitro.config.ts`).

### a) Per-share HTML with injected meta — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].ts`
- Register a route `"/s/**" → server/routes/s/[id].ts` in `nitro.config.ts` `handlers` (takes precedence over the `"/**": { static: true }` SPA fallback).
- Read the share via `useStorage("keating:share").getItem(id)` (same store written by `server/api/share/index.ts`). Pull `title`, derive a `description` from the first user/assistant message text (truncated, sanitized).
- Read the built SPA shell (`dist/index.html`) — via Nitro public-asset storage or `fs.readFile`. Replace/append the OG + Twitter meta tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:url` = the share URL, `og:image` = `/s/:id/og.png`, `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:title/description/image`) and `<title>`. Return the HTML (`text/html`).
- If the share is missing, fall through to the normal SPA shell (so the client renders its own 404). Escape all interpolated text to avoid meta-tag injection.

### b) Per-share image — new handler `web/server/routes/s/[id].og.png.ts`
- Render with **satori** (already in `node_modules/satori`) → SVG, then **@resvg/resvg-wasm** (`node_modules/@resvg/resvg-wasm`) → PNG (1200×630). Mirror the visual language of `web/src/og-image.tsx` (dark terminal card, accent colors) but inject the share's `title` + a short snippet.
- Satori needs a font buffer: add one TTF under `web/server/assets/fonts/` (e.g. Inter/DejaVu Sans) and load it via Nitro server assets / `fs`. resvg-wasm needs its wasm initialized once (cache the module + font in module scope).
- Cache the rendered PNG per id in `useStorage("keating:share-og")` (or an in-memory LRU) to avoid re-rendering on every crawl. Set `Cache-Control: public, max-age=...` and `Content-Type: image/png`.
- Add matching `routeRules`/headers if needed so the `.png` isn't caught by the SPA fallback.

### c) Share URL
- Confirm `SharedSession` route is `/s/$shareId` (`web/src/App.tsx:73`). The OG `og:url`/canonical should point at the absolute `https://<host>/s/:id`. Derive host from the request (`getRequestURL(event)`).

---

## Verification

- `cd web && bun run typecheck` and `bun test` (existing suites: error classification, ui-settings, speech). Add focused unit tests: `classifyError` billing case; `resolveSpeechCredential` precedence (OpenAI over Google; ignores absence); `speechInputMode` mapping.
- **Fix #3/#4**: with a configured generator that returns 402/insufficient_quota, `generate_image` shows the classified billing `ErrorBadge` (not plain text); the no-key case still shows the plain "no image generator available" message.
- **Fix #5**: trigger a 401 from a provider, click *Re-enter API key*, enter a valid key → the failed turn re-runs and completes; verify the key is stored under the real provider (not `unknown`).
- **Speech**: with only a Google key, mic icon appears and STT fills the composer; with OpenAI Realtime selected + key, the icon opens a live session; with no speech credential, the icon is hidden.
- **OG**: `curl -A "Discordbot" https://<host>/s/<id>` returns HTML whose `og:title`/`og:description` match the share; open `/s/<id>/og.png` and confirm a per-share card renders; validate the embed in a Discord message / a card validator.
- Run `bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts` after editing `browser-tools.ts`.
